description: Automatically steals all items from chests. Supports configurable delays, auto-closing, and name-based chest filtering.

fields.delay: int - tick counter for delay between shift-click operations, incremented when items remain in chest
fields.startDelay: int - tick counter for initial delay before any stealing begins

settings.name: Start Delay (Ticks)
settings.description: Ticks to wait after opening chest before stealing

settings.name: Auto Close
settings.description: Automatically close the chest when all items are taken

settings.name: Custom Chest
settings.description: If false, only steals from chests named 'Chest', 'Large Chest', or 'Ender Chest'. If true, steals from any container regardless of name.

onPreMotion.start_delay: If startDelay <= Start Delay setting value, increment startDelay and return early (wait before stealing)
onPreMotion.custom_chest_check: If Custom Chest is OFF: verify the chest name is exactly 'Chest', 'Large Chest', or 'Ender Chest' via getLowerChestInventory().getName()

stealing.action: If slot has a non-null item stack: set chestEmpty=false, then if delay >= Delay setting: mc.playerController.windowClick(windowId, slotIndex, 0, 1, mc.thePlayer) [SHIFT_CLICK to move to player inventory], set delay=0
stealing.tick_increment: If chest is not empty after iterating all slots: delay++

auto_close.condition: All slots in chest are empty (chestEmpty=true at end of loop)
auto_close.action: If Auto Close is enabled: mc.thePlayer.closeScreen()

chest_name_validation.note: This check is case-sensitive and only runs when Custom Chest setting is false. When Custom Chest is true, any container name is accepted.

minecraft: net.minecraft.init.Blocks (used in import but not referenced in code)

implementation_notes: The module uses shift-click (mode=1) to move items from the chest into the player's inventory in one click.

Delay is measured in game ticks (20 ticks = 1 second). Start Delay and Delay are separate: start delay only counts at the beginning, per-item delay resets after each shift-click and increments until reaching the target.
The loop iterates ALL slots in the chest container, not just non-empty ones. Empty slots are skipped.
ChestEmpty is determined by checking getLowerChestInventory().getSizeInventory() slots - this is the bottom/fixed portion of a double chest container. The variable name inventory counts all slots.
Auto-close uses mc.thePlayer.closeScreen() which sends a C0DPacketCloseWindow to the server.
This module does NOT reference any other client modules (unlike KillAura or NoSlow which cross-reference each other). It's self-contained.
GuiChest is only imported but not used in any instanceof check - the openContainer instanceof ContainerChest check is sufficient.
